title: 3 Medical Routines That Older People May Not Need
summary: Some screenings and treatments no longer make sense for patients as they age. Researchers have just added a few more to the list.

title: Trump’s $50B Rural Health Bet Meets a Healthcare Desert in North Carolina
summary: Republicans promise that $50 billion in new health funding will help rural America. But it’s not expected to aid the years-long effort in North Carolina’s Martin County to reopen its only hospital.

title: ICE Arrests Are Separating Families. Here’s How To Plan Ahead.
summary: As immigration authorities carry out President Donald Trump’s promise to conduct what’s billed as the largest mass deportation operation in U.S. history, several states are passing laws to protect the children of detained immigrants. Guardianship can become complicated when no family or friends are available to take temporary custody.

title: Colorado Charts Its Own Course on Vaccines Amid Federal Pullback
summary: Doctors, lawmakers, and other advocates are joining forces to promote recommended childhood vaccines.

title: Religious Anti-Abortion Center Finds Opportunity in Town Without OB-GYNs
summary: A crisis pregnancy center in Sandpoint, Idaho, wants to expand women’s healthcare three years after the labor and delivery unit at the town’s hospital closed and its OB-GYNs moved out of state.

title: Watch: The Tug-of-War Over Taxpayer Dollars
summary: Podcast host Julie Rovner chats with Sen. Tammy Baldwin, a top Democrat on health issues, about President Donald Trump’s stewardship of federal spending and the effectiveness of the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline.

title: Listen to the Latest ‘KFF Health News Minute’
summary: The "KFF Health News Minute” brings original health care and health policy reporting from our newsroom to the airwaves each week.

title: Efforts To Understand the Nation’s Drugged Driving Problem Stall Under Trump
summary: The data behind alcohol-related traffic deaths is well studied. Less understood is the toll of vehicle deaths involving drugs or a combination of drugs and alcohol. Attempts to fix that have been stymied by federal budget and staffing cuts.

title: Eroding ACA Enrollment Portends Higher Insurance Rates
summary: An uptick in people skipping Obamacare premium payments in many states suggests the Affordable Care Act’s rising costs — driven partly by lower subsidies to help people buy plans — are hitting home for 2026 enrollees. The trend adds to voter concerns about affordability ahead of the midterm elections.

title: A Danish Couple’s Maverick African Research Finds Its Moment in RFK Jr.’s Vaccine Policy
summary: The work of Peter Aaby and Christine Stabell Benn has long been controversial. Until Robert F. Kennedy Jr. became the U.S. health policy chief, most vaccine scientists tended to ignore it. That has changed.
